bullets: Win32-native IDE shell with integrated terminals, agent-mirrored output, and tool guardrails — not a generic chat wrapper.
Inference stack can pair with local runners; product differentiation is the editor + agent loop + native UX velocity.
PDF and charts below are generated from this JSON via scripts/Export-MetricsDeck.ps1 — re-run after benchmarks to refresh.

competitors.framing: Ollama and vLLM are not substitutes for an IDE — they are complementary infrastructure. RawrXD owns the developer surface; runners own throughput and model serving.

rows.name: Ollama
rows.overlap: Pull/run GGUF; chat API; desktop app
rows.rawrxdAngle: Wire Ollama as one backend; RawrXD adds Win32 IDE, agent tools, terminal parity, and enterprise guardrails.

rows.name: vLLM
rows.rawrxdAngle: Optional remote backend for high-TPS workloads; IDE still controls tools, workspace trust, and audit trails.

rows.name: RawrXD
rows.position: Native agentic IDE + optional local/remote inference
rows.overlap: None as a pure server — product is the shell
rows.rawrxdAngle: Ship Cursor/VS Code-class terminal UX with read-only agent panes, bounded loops, and integrated tool echo.
